zh.lifeSummary: 當Berenguela de León於1204年出生在西班牙卡斯利亞 Y 利安利安利安時，她的父親Alfonso De León IX33歲，母親Berenguela de Castilla Reina de Castilla si reina consorte de León24歲。她於1224年5月與King John 'the Crusader' de Brienne I結婚。他們至少是4個兒子和2個女兒的父母。她去世於1237年4月12日在土耳其易斯坦波爾享年33歲，埋葬在西班牙加利西亞A 可盧納桑亞格 德 可波斯特拉。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Berenguela de León于1204年出生在西班牙卡斯利亞 Y 利安利安利安时，她的父亲Alfonso De León IX33岁，母亲Berenguela de Castilla Reina de Castilla si reina consorte de León24岁。她于1224年5月与King John 'the Crusader' de Brienne I结婚。他们至少是4个儿子和2个女儿的父母。她去世于1237年4月12日在土耳其易斯坦波爾享年33岁，埋葬在西班牙加利西亞A 可盧納桑亞格 德 可波斯特拉。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: León
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Juan, Luis, Manuel, Carlos, Pedro, Jesus, Jorge, Francisco, Rafael, Miguel, Raul.

Spanish (León): habitational name from León, a city in northwestern Spain, named with Latin legio, genitive legionis ‘legion’, a division of the Roman army. In Roman times the city was the garrison of the 7th Legion, known as the Legio Gemina. The city's name became shortened from Legion(em) to Leon(em), and in this form developed an unetymological association with the word for ‘lion’ (Spanish león).

French (Léon) and Spanish (León): from the personal name Léon, León, from Latin leo ‘lion’, or the cognate Greek leōn. The name Leo(n) was borne by numerous early Christian martyrs and thirteen popes.

variants.name: Len
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Polish Zbigniew, Jozef, Karol.
Polish and Jewish (eastern Ashkenazic): metonymic occupational name for a flax grower or merchant, from Polish len ‘flax’.
Polish (Leń): nickname from leń ‘lazybones’.

variants.name: Leonides
meanings: Hispanic (Leónides; Mexico): from the personal name Leónides, from Greek Leonidēs, itself most likely a patronymic derivative of Leōn (from leōn ‘lion’; see Leon ) + the suffix -(e)ides.

variants.name: Lyon
meanings: Scottish, English, and French: from the personal name Middle English Lyon, Old French Leon (from Latin leo ‘lion’, or the cognate Greek leōn; see Leon ). Compare Lyall .
Scottish and English (of Norman origin): habitational name from Lyons-la-Forêt in Eure, Normandy. It is unlikely to be from the better-known southern French city of Lyon (see 5 below).

English and French: nickname from Middle English lioun ‘lion’ (Old English, Latin leo), Old French leon, perhaps applied to a brave, fierce, or proud person, or one with a shaggy mane of hair. Compare Lion .

variants.name: Leos
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Pedro, Francisco, Manuel, Ruben, Carlos, Juan, Santos, Jesus, Pablo, Raul, Alfonso.
Hispanic (mainly Mexico): altered form of Basque Leoz, itself a Castilianized form (also Léoz) of a habitational name from Leotz (in Castilian: Leoz) in Navarre.
Greek: from the personal name Leos, a pet form of Leon .

variants.name: Liang
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Hong, Jian, Feng, Wen, Wei, Ping, Ying, Cheng, Guang, Jin, Jing, Liang, Chang, Chung, Hu, Nam, Pang, Jang, Min, Neng, Shen, Shiu, Sieu, Yeong.
Chinese:

Mandarin form of the surname 梁, meaning ‘roofbeam’ or ‘bridge’ in Chinese: (i) from Liang (梁), the name of a state (located in present-day Hancheng in Shaanxi Province) which was granted to Kang, the youngest son of Qinzhong (died 822  BC ), because the latter made great contributions to the fight against minority ethnic groups in western China during the reign of King Ping of Zhou (died 720  BC ). After the state was annihilated by the state of Qin in 641  BC , some people kept the name of the state as their surname. (ii) from the second element of the placenames Jie Liang (解梁), Gao Liang (高粱), and Qu Liang (曲梁), names of several cities originally within the border of the state of Jin (located mainly in present-day Shanxi province). In 645  BC , the Duke Hui of Jin (ruler of the state of Jin, died 637  BC ) ceded these cities to the state of Qin (located originally in present-day western Shaanxi province). Later, people from these cities adopted 粱 as their surname. (iii) from Liang (梁), also known as South Liang, the name of another state (located in present-day Longxi in Gansu Province) which was granted to Tang, son of King Ping of Zhou (died 720  BC ). After this state was annihilated by the state of Chu during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ), some people kept the name of the state as their surname. (iii) from the Ba Lie Lan (拔列蘭) family, members of an ethnic group in northern China, who changed their original surname to 梁 during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ).

variants.name: Leonti
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Domenic, Salvatore.
Italian (Sicily): from the Greek personal name or nickname Leon, Leontos ‘lion’ (equivalent of Latin Leo, Leonis; see Leon 2).

variants.name: Lew
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Wing, Yuen, Fook, Jung, Ying, Young, Yung, Bin, Foon, Hong, Kam, Koon.
Polish: from the personal name Lew ‘lion’, adopted as a translation of Leon .
Jewish (Ashkenazic): variant of   Lev  .

en.lifeSummary: When Berenguela de León was born in 1204, in León, León, Castilla y León, Spain, her father, Alfonso De León IX, was 33 and her mother, Berenguela de Castilla Reina de Castilla si reina consorte de León, was 24. She married King John 'the Crusader' de Brienne I in May 1224. They were the parents of at least 4 sons and 2 daughters. She died on 12 April 1237, in İstanbul, Türkiye, at the age of 33, and was buried in Santiago de Compostela, A Coruña, Galicia, Spain.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Berenguela de León est née en 1204, à León, León, Castilla y León, Espagne, son père, Alfonso De León IX, avait 33 ans et sa mère, Berenguela de Castilla Reina de Castilla si reina consorte de León, avait 24 ans. Elle a épousé King John 'the Crusader' de Brienne I en mai 1224. Ils ont eu au moins 4 fils et 2 filles. Elle est décédée le 12 avril 1237, à İstanbul, Turquie, à l’âge de 33 ans, et a été enterrée à Santiago de Compostela, A Coruña, Galicia, Espagne.

de.lifeSummary: Als Berenguela de León 1204 in León, León, Castilla y León, Spanien geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Alfonso De León IX 33 und ihre Mutter Berenguela de Castilla Reina de Castilla si reina consorte de León 24. Sie hat im Mai 1224 King John 'the Crusader' de Brienne I geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 4 Söhne und 2 Töchter. Sie starb am 12. April 1237 im Alter von 33 Jahren in İstanbul, Türkei und wurde in Santiago de Compostela, A Coruña, Galicia, Spanien begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Berenguela de León è nata il nel 1204 nel León, León, Castilla y León, Spagna, sua padre, Alfonso De León IX, aveva 33 anni e sua madre, Berenguela de Castilla Reina de Castilla si reina consorte de León, aveva 24 anni. Lei sposato King John 'the Crusader' de Brienne I nel maggio 1224. Erano genitori di almeno 4 figli e 2 figlie. Morì il 12 aprile 1237, nel İstanbul, Turchia, all'età di 33 anni, e fu sepolta a Santiago de Compostela, A Coruña, Galicia, Spagna.

ko.lifeSummary: Berenguela de León(이)가 1204년 스페인 카스틸라 와이 레o 레o 레o에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Alfonso De León IX(은)는 33세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Berenguela de Castilla Reina de Castilla si reina consorte de León(은)는 24세였다. 그녀는 1224년 5월 King John 'the Crusader' de Brienne I(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 네 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1237년 4월 12일 튀르키예 이스탠불에서 33세의 나이로 사망했으며 스페인 갈리샤 에이 코루n아 산티아고 데 콤포스텔아에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Berenguela de León nasceu em 1204, em León, León, Castilla y León, Espanha, seu pai, Alfonso De León IX, tinha 33 anos e sua mãe, Berenguela de Castilla Reina de Castilla si reina consorte de León, tinha 24 anos. Ela casou-se com King John 'the Crusader' de Brienne I em maio de 1224. Eles tiveram pelo menos 4 filhos e 2 filhas. Ela faleceu em 12 de abril de 1237, em İstanbul, Turquia, com 33 anos, e foi sepultada em Santiago de Compostela, A Coruña, Galicia, Espanha.

ru.Santiago de Compostela, A Coruña, Galici: сантяго де компостела, а коруnа, галися, Испания
ru.lifeSummary: Когда Berenguela de León родилась в 1204 г. в местности леoн, леoн, кастилла и леoн, Испания, её отцу по имени Alfonso De León IX было 33 года, а матери, которую звали Berenguela de Castilla Reina de Castilla si reina consorte de León, 24. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени King John 'the Crusader' de Brienne I май 1224 г. У них было по крайней мере 4 сына и 2 дочери. Она умерла 12 апреля 1237 г. в местности истанбул, Турция в возрасте 33 лет и была похоронена в местности сантяго де компостела, а коруnа, галися, Испания.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Berenguela de León nació en 1204, en León, León, Castilla y León, España, su papá, Alfonso De León IX, tenía 33 años y su mamá, Berenguela de Castilla Reina de Castilla si reina consorte de León, tenía 24 años. Se casó con King John 'the Crusader' de Brienne I en mayo de 1224. Fueron padres de por lo menos 4 hijos y 2 hijas. Murió el 12 de abril de 1237, en İstanbul, Turquía, a la edad de 33 años, y fue sepultada en Santiago de Compostela, La Coruña, Galicia, España.
